algorithms.html: The following algorithm specifies how to encode a verifiable credential into a text string that can be expressed in a QR Code. Required inputs are a verifiable credential (map inputDocument), and a set of options (map options). The output is an encoded verifiable credential (string) or an error. Whenever this algorithm encodes strings, it MUST use UTF-8 encoding.

steps.html: Let <var>typeTable</var> be the value of <var>options</var>.<var>typeTable</var>.
Let <var>registryEntryId</var> be the value of <var>options</var>.<var>registryEntryId</var>.
Let cborldDocument be the result of the JSON-LD to CBOR-LD Encoding Algorithm, passing inputDocument, typeTable, and registryEntryId as inputs. If an encoding error occurs, an error MUST be raised.
Let base45Value be the result of the Base Encoding algorithm, passing cborldDocument as bytes, the integer 45 as targetBase, and 0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ $%*+-./: as the baseAlphabet.
Let qrCodeText be the result of concatenating the following values: VC1-, R (the Multibase prefix for base45), and base45Value.
Return qrCodeText as the encoded verifiable credential, which can then be provided to any QR Code library as text to be encoded as a QR Code image.

algorithms.html: The following algorithm specifies how to decode a verifiable credential that has been encoded into a QR Code. Required inputs are a text string (string inputDocument), and a set of options (map options). The output is a verifiable credential (map) or an error. Whenever this algorithm encodes strings, it MUST use UTF-8 encoding.

steps.html: Ensure that the inputDocument starts with the text VC1-R. If it does not an error MUST be raised.
Let base45Value be inputDocument with the first five characters (VC1-R) removed.

Let cborldDocument be the result of the Base Decoding algorithm, passing base45Value as sourceEncoding, the integer 45 as sourceBase, and 0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ $%*+-./: as the baseAlphabet.
Let jsonldDocument be the result of CBOR-LD to JSON-LD Decoding Algorithm, passing cborldDocument as the input. If a decoding error occurs, an error MUST be raised.
Return jsonldDocument as the decoded verifiable credential.
Set <var>opticalData</var> to the data in the optical barcode to be secured.
Set <var>issuerUrl</var> to the URL the issuer wishes to use for credential verification.
Set unsignedCredential to an OpticalBarcodeCredential with unsignedCredential.issuer set to issuerUrl.
If credential status is required:
Set statusListEntry to the BitstringStatusListEntry (as defined in the Bitstring Status List v1.0 specification) that the issuer wishes to add to the OpticalBarcodeCredential.
Set unsignedCredential.credentialStatus to the result of applying the algorithm in Section 3.2.3.2 BitstringStatusListEntry to TerseBitstringStatusListEntry to statusListEntry.
Set signedCredential to the result of using the algorithm in 3.2.4.1 Add Proof (ecdsa-xi-2023) to sign opticalData and unsignedCredential.

Encode signedCredential using CBOR-LD [CBOR-LD] and add it to the designated area of the opticalData in accordance with the processing rules for the desired value of credentialSubject.type and the use case.

Set <var>opticalData</var> to the machine-readable data (e.g., the PDF417 or MRZ data)
scanned from the physical document.
Set vcbPayload to the verifiable credential payload extracted from the designated area of opticalData.

Set cborldBytes to the result of decoding vcbPayload from the character encoding associated with the credential type and barcode format (see Section 2.3 Encoding to and from barcodes), removing any multibase header if one is present.

Set credential to the OpticalBarcodeCredential that results from applying CBOR-LD decompression [CBOR-LD] to cborldBytes, if the payload is CBOR-LD compressed; otherwise, set credential to the OpticalBarcodeCredential expressed by cborldBytes.
Set verificationResult to the result of applying the algorithm in Section 3.2.4.2 Verify Proof (ecdsa-xi-2023) to credential and opticalData.
Set <var>statusResult</var> to null. If <var>credential</var>.<var>credentialStatus</var> is present:
Set statusListEntry to the result of applying the algorithm in Section 3.2.3.1 TerseBitstringStatusListEntry to BitstringStatusListEntry to credential.
Set statusResult to the result of applying the algorithm in Bitstring Status List v1.0: Validate Algorithm to statusListEntry.
Return (<var>verificationResult</var>, <var>statusResult</var>).

algorithms.html: After verifiable credential verification has been performed, the algorithm takes an OpticalBarcodeCredential verifiable credential (struct vc), an integer listLength containing the number of entries in the BitstringStatusListCredential associated with vc, and a string statusPurpose (e.g., 'revocation', 'suspension'...) as input and returns a 'BitstringStatusListEntry' object.

steps.html: Set result to be an empty map.
Set listIndex to vc.credentialStatus.terseStatusListIndex/listLength rounded down to the next lowest integer (i.e., apply the floor() operation).
Set <var>statusListIndex</var> to <var>vc</var>.<var>credentialStatus</var>.<var>terseStatusListIndex</var> % <var>listLength</var>.

Set <var>result</var>.<var>statusListCredential</var> to the concatenation of the following values:
<var>vc</var>.<var>credentialStatus</var>.<var>terseStatusListBaseUrl</var>, '/', <var>statusPurpose</var>, '/', <var>listIndex</var>.
Set <var>result</var>.<var>type</var> to 'BitstringStatusListEntry'.
Set <var>result</var>.<var>statusListIndex</var> to <var>statusListIndex</var>.
Set <var>result</var>.<var>statusPurpose</var> to <var>statusPurpose</var>.
Return <var>result</var>.

algorithms.html: The algorithm takes a BitstringStatusListEntry (struct statusListEntry), whose statusListCredential value is expected to be a URL of the form terseStatusListBaseUrl, '/', statusPurpose, '/', listIndex, and an integer listLength containing the number of entries in each BitstringStatusListCredential published by the issuer as input, and returns a 'TerseBitstringStatusListEntry' object.

steps.html: Set result to be an empty map.

Set <var>listIndex</var> to the integer representation of the final path segment of
<var>statusListEntry</var>.<var>statusListCredential</var>.

Set <var>terseStatusListBaseUrl</var> to <var>statusListEntry</var>.<var>statusListCredential</var>
with the final two path segments (i.e., '/', <var>statusPurpose</var>, '/',
<var>listIndex</var>) removed.
Set <var>result</var>.<var>type</var> to 'TerseBitstringStatusListEntry'.
Set <var>result</var>.<var>terseStatusListBaseUrl</var> to <var>terseStatusListBaseUrl</var>.

Set <var>result</var>.<var>terseStatusListIndex</var> to <var>listIndex</var> * <var>listLength</var> + the
integer representation of <var>statusListEntry</var>.<var>statusListIndex</var>.
Return <var>result</var>.

algorithms.html: The hashing algorithm is what is defined in the Hashing (ecdsa-rdfc-2019) section of the Data Integrity ECDSA Cryptosuites v1.0 with step 3 replaced with the following two steps:

steps.html:
Let <var>opticalDataHash</var> be the result of applying the same hash algorithm
that was applied to <var>hashData</var> to the <var>opticalDataBytes</var>
value.

Let <var>hashData</var> be the result of joining <var>proofConfigHash</var> (the
first hash), <var>transformedDocumentHash</var> (the second hash), and
<var>opticalDataHash</var> (the third hash).

algorithms.html:
The required inputs to this algorithm are the machine-readable optical data
(<var>opticalData</var>) and the credential subject (<var>credentialSubject</var>). A single
value representing the hashed canonical form of the protected optical data
is produced as output.

steps.html: Set dataToCanonicalize to the result of extracting the protected components of opticalData as an array of strings, according to the component extraction rules associated with the type of credentialSubject, using credentialSubject.protectedComponentIndex if it is defined for that type. The component extraction rules for MachineReadableZone are given in Section Component extraction: MachineReadableZone; the rules for other types are given in the specifications or appendices that define them (e.g., Appendix A.2 Component Extraction for the type defined in Appendix A. AAMVA Driver License and Identification Documents, and Appendix B.2 Component Extraction for the type described in Appendix B. ISO-Compliant Driving Licences).

Set <var>canonicalizedData</var> to the result of sorting <var>dataToCanonicalize</var> into a
deterministic list, using the ordering rules associated with the type of
<var>credentialSubject</var>, and then applying a join operation to create a single
string from the array. If the type of <var>credentialSubject</var> does not define
ordering rules, sort <var>dataToCanonicalize</var> in Unicode code point order.
Hash <var>canonicalizedData</var> and return the result.

algorithms.html: For a credentialSubject of type MachineReadableZone, opticalData is the data in the machine-readable zone, and the protected components are extracted as follows. The ordering rules for this type are the order in which the lines appear on the credential.

steps.html: Set <var>dataToCanonicalize</var> to an empty array.
For each line in the Machine Readable Zone on the credential:
Set <var>mrzLine</var> to a string containing the data in that line.
Append a newline character to the end of <var>mrzLine</var> and append <var>mrzLine</var> to <var>dataToCanonicalize</var>.
Return <var>dataToCanonicalize</var>.

algorithms.html: The proof configuration algorithm is what is defined in the Proof Configuration (ecdsa-rdfc-2019) section of the Data Integrity ECDSA Cryptosuites v1.0 with step 4 replaced with the following step:

steps.html: If options.type is not set to DataIntegrityProof and proofConfig.cryptosuite is not set to ecdsa-xi-2023, an INVALID_PROOF_CONFIGURATION error MUST be raised.

algorithms.html: For a credentialSubject of type AamvaDriversLicenseScannableInformation, opticalData is the data in the PDF417 barcode, and the protected components are extracted as follows. The ordering rules for this type are the default Unicode code point order.

steps.html: Set <var>dataToCanonicalize</var> to an empty array.
Set bitfieldDecoded to be first 22 bits of the length 24 bitstring resulting from decoding credentialSubject.protectedComponentIndex from multibase-base64url to binary.

Set fieldsAlphabetized to be the deterministic list of components for this type, i.e., an array containing the 22 AAMVA mandatory PDF417 Element IDs [aamva-dl-id-card-design-standard] sorted in Unicode code point order (i.e., ['DAC', 'DAD' ... 'DDG']).
For each bit with value 1 in bitfieldDecoded:
Set the string <var>fieldName</var> to <var>fieldsAlphabetized</var>[<var>i</var>], where <var>i</var> is the index of the bit in <var>bitfieldDecoded</var>.
Set the string <var>fieldData</var> to the data that will be in the PDF417 associated with that field name.
Concatenate fieldData to the end of fieldName, concatenate a newline character (\n, U+000A) to the end, and append the result to dataToCanonicalize.
Return <var>dataToCanonicalize</var>.
